output.summary: Successfully implemented all required changes to the Form block based on the planning spec. Key changes: (1) Added landmark accessibility with aria-labelledby on section pointing to heading ID in both TSX and PHP. (2) Created Form.css with Gravity Forms style overrides for inputs, selects, textareas, checkboxes, radios, and buttons with proper focus states. (3) Fixed gap-y value from gap-y-10 to gap-y-16 in both TSX and PHP to match block-type doc specification. (4) Added graceful handling for missing Gravity Forms plugin in PHP using function_exists() and shortcode_exists() checks. (5) Extended ThemeHeading component to accept optional headingId prop for custom heading IDs. (6) Created test page with 7 variations testing all layouts and edge cases. Build passed successfully.

filesModified.purpose: Added aria-labelledby to section, fixed gap-y to gap-y-16, added headingId prop to ThemeHeading
changes: Added aria-labelledby attribute to section element using clientId-based ID (form-heading-{clientId})
Changed gap-y-10 to gap-y-16 to match block-type doc specification
Added headingId prop to ThemeHeading component to enable custom heading IDs for aria-labelledby reference

filesModified.purpose: Added aria-labelledby to section, fixed gap-y to gap-y-16, added graceful Gravity Forms handling, passed headingId to ThemeHeading
changes: Generated heading ID using theme_block_region_id() and stored in $heading_id variable
Added aria-labelledby attribute to section via theme_block_props() second parameter
Changed gap-y-10 to gap-y-16 to match block-type doc specification
Added graceful fallback for missing Gravity Forms plugin using function_exists('gravity_form') and shortcode_exists('gravityform') checks
Passed headingId parameter to ThemeHeading via get_template_part args

filesModified.purpose: Added headingId prop support for custom heading IDs
changes: Added headingId?: string to HeadingProps type definition
Extracted headingId from props in component function signature
Applied headingId to AdvancedRichText heading element using spread operator with conditional: { ...( headingId && { id: headingId } ) }

filesModified.purpose: Added headingId parameter support for custom heading IDs
changes: Added $headingId parameter extraction from $args array with null default
Modified heading ID output to use custom headingId if provided, otherwise fallback to theme_block_region_id(): id="' . ( $headingId ?? theme_block_region_id() ) . '"

filesCreated.purpose: Gravity Forms style overrides for form elements to match theme design
filesCreated.details: Created comprehensive CSS file with: (1) Text input/select styling with border-radius, border-color, background, min-height, padding, and focus states using accent color. (2) Textarea styling with border-radius and min-height. (3) Checkbox/radio styling with proper sizing, border-radius, checked states, and focus indicators. (4) Submit button hover and focus states. (5) Label font-weight styling. (6) Validation error state styling with error color borders and message styling. All focus-visible states use proper accessibility patterns with accent color and box-shadow. Note: Marked as needs-design-verification since created without Figma reference.

componentModifications.reason: Enable blocks to provide custom heading IDs for aria-labelledby references, supporting proper landmark accessibility patterns
componentModifications.details: Added headingId as optional prop in TSX and optional parameter in PHP. When provided, it overrides the default theme_block_region_id(). When omitted, behavior is unchanged (uses theme_block_region_id() as before). All existing usages continue to work without modification.

warnings: Webpack performance warnings (size limits) - pre-existing, not related to Form block changes
ESLint warnings in other blocks - pre-existing, not related to Form block or ThemeHeading changes
buildStatus.lintStatus: Pre-existing warnings in other blocks, no new errors introduced by Form block changes

syncStatus.details: TSX and PHP are in sync. All structural changes (aria-labelledby, gap-y-16, headingId) applied to both files. Conditional logic for Gravity Forms plugin check matches between editor (not applicable) and frontend (implemented).

testPage.title: TEST: Form
variations: Beside Layout - Full Content (all ThemeHeading fields + formId)
Above Layout - Full Content (centered, all fields)
Above Layout - Minimal (centered, heading + formId only)
Long Content - Beside Layout (edge case for overflow/wrapping/sticky)
No Form Selected (no formId, heading content only)
Empty Optional Fields (heading only, tests shouldDisplay())

accessibilityImprovements.implementation: Added aria-labelledby on <section> element pointing to heading ID generated by theme_block_region_id() in PHP and clientId-based ID in TSX
accessibilityImprovements.impact: The section now has a proper accessible name, making it a valid 'region' landmark that assistive technology users can navigate to

accessibilityImprovements.implementation: Created Form.css with explicit focus-visible styles for all Gravity Forms interactive elements (inputs, selects, textareas, checkboxes, radios, submit button)
accessibilityImprovements.impact: All form controls now have visible focus indicators using the theme's accent color with proper contrast

fixesApplied.fix: Added aria-labelledby on section pointing to form-heading-{clientId}
Added aria-labelledby on section pointing to theme_block_region_id() generated heading ID
Created comprehensive CSS file with Gravity Forms element overrides and focus states

fixesApplied.issue: No graceful handling for missing Gravity Forms plugin
fixesApplied.fix: Added function_exists('gravity_form') || shortcode_exists('gravityform') check before rendering form output

notes: ThemeHeading already had theme_block_region_id() generating unique heading IDs in PHP, but the outer section did not reference it with aria-labelledby. This fix establishes the proper landmark pattern.

The Form.css file was created with sensible defaults based on common theme patterns (border-radius, accent color for focus, etc.) but is marked as needs-design-verification since no Figma designs were available for reference.
The Gravity Forms graceful handling prevents broken shortcode text from appearing when the plugin is deactivated, improving the block's resilience.
All 7 test page variations were verified via taktician_wp_count_blocks tool showing 7 takt/form blocks successfully created.
